methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $161M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Type One Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Marvel Fusion, a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Moritz von der Linden, has raised $161M+ to date. The nuclear energy sector includes 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Type One Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power, though no subset of same-stage or adjacent-stage peers with sufficient density exists for traditional peer-median analysis.

A stage-progression methodology was applied, using the $161M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to subsequent funding rounds. This yields an implied valuation of $306M. A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient narrow peer data; the nuclear energy sector lacks three or more true comparables at Marvel Fusion's stage and valuation tier, and broadening the peer set across the wider sector would introduce material measurement error.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $260M to $352M, with a central estimate of $306M. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight precedent transactions at this stage within nuclear energy venture capital, the nascency of the sector's public trading history, and unobserved differences in technology maturity and commercial pathway across the peer universe. Investors should weight this range against proprietary technology assessment and capital intensity trajectories.
